input.content:
# Plan: normalizeOrderEvent Implementation

## Context
Bird has defined 6 acceptance criteria for a `normalizeOrderEvent` TypeScript function. MJ has specified the types and architectural constraints. No existing codebase — standalone implementation goes to `.tmp/`.

## Files to Create

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `.tmp/normalizeOrderEvent.ts` | Implementation + type definitions |
| `.tmp/normalizeOrderEvent.test.ts` | Jest unit tests covering all 6 ACs |
| `.tmp/package.json` | Minimal config: ts-jest, jest |
| `.tmp/tsconfig.json` | Minimal TS config for strict mode |

## Implementation Approach

### Types (in `normalizeOrderEvent.ts`)
```ts
interface RawOrderEvent {
  id: string;
  status: string;
  total_amount: number;
  customer_name?: string | null;
}

interface NormalizedOrderEvent {
  id: string;
  status: "completed" | "refunded" | "failed" | "unknown";
  amount: number;
  customer_name: string;
}

interface Logger {
  warn(message: string): void;
}
```

### Status Mapping (AC1–AC4)
- `"PAID"` → `"completed"`
- `"REFUNDED"` → `"refunded"`
- `"FAILED"` | `"DECLINED"` → `"failed"`
- anything else → `"unknown"` + `logger.warn(...)`

### Amount Conversion (AC5)
`amount = parseFloat((raw.total_amount / 100).toFixed(2))`

### Customer Name (AC6)
`customer_name = raw.customer_name ?? "Anonymous"` (also handle empty null/undefined)

## Test Coverage
Each AC gets its own `it(...)` block. Logger is a jest mock object `{ warn: jest.fn() }`.

| Test | AC |
|------|----|
| status PAID → completed | AC1 |
| status REFUNDED → refunded | AC2 |
| status FAILED → failed | AC3 |
| status DECLINED → failed | AC3 |
| status PROCESSING → unknown + warn | AC4 |
| total_amount 1099 → 10.99 | AC5 |
| total_amount 100 → 1.00 | AC5 |
| customer_name missing → "Anonymous" | AC6 |
| customer_name null → "Anonymous" | AC6 |
| customer_name present → preserved | AC6 (positive) |

## Verification
```bash
cd .tmp && npm install && npx jest normalizeOrderEvent.test.ts
```

All 10 tests should pass.
